body.title: [sprint] Skills-why auditor — standing why-completeness check (cheap filter, escalate on hit)
body.description:
**Problem:** 'Every skill carries a coherent why' is a STANDING property that drifts every time a skill is added/edited — so it needs a continuous observer, not a one-shot backfill. It is the second built instance needed to distill the always-on observer pattern (T-always-on-observer-pattern); T-build-log-digester (closed) is the first.
**Proposed shape:** A standing auditor over the skills corpus that flags any skill missing the `Why:` format (per T-skill-why-convention) as a drift/debris signal. CHEAP filter first — presence/structure check is inference-free; escalate to an LLM coherence judgment ONLY on flagged candidates (never run inference on clean skills). First run inventories the corpus (subsumes the one-shot backfill); thereafter runs continuously. Home in Scraps (non-inference maintenance) per the device roster.
**Affected files:** NEW skills-why auditor (devices/scraps/jobs/ or similar); reads skills/; emits a queryable drift signal. Inference via the Inference Proxy ONLY, on the escalation path.
**Design rules:** Inference-Proxy-only; three-tier cost (cheap filter; escalate on hit; NEVER expensive brain on watch); External State Principle (drift projection durable); free cloud LLM stays on 'where to look', a captured/flagged why is confirmed by Akien or a judge, never auto-blessed.
**Scope boundary:** IN — presence-check filter over skills + drift-signal emission + the escalation hook (one candidate path). OUT — auto-writing whys without review; the generalized cross-corpus contract (that is T-always-on-observer-pattern); rewriting T-why-sorter.
**Completion criteria:** A why-less test skill produces a queryable drift signal; clean skills produce ZERO inference calls (assert via a stubbed inference-client call-count == 0); the escalation-on-candidate path is exercised at least once; first run inventories the existing skills corpus.
**Test plan:** Drop a why-less fixture skill -> drift signal emitted + queryable. Annotate it -> signal clears. Run over a clean set with a stubbed inference client -> call-count is 0. Force one candidate -> escalation path invoked.
